nodes.name: 淡水沼澤森林
nodes.summary: 淡水沼澤森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括Sunda coucal、Western striolated puffbird、Eastern striolated puffbird等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 圭亚那 (地区)
nodes.summary: 圭亚那 (地区)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联92种鸟类，包括Blue-throated piping guan、Black curassow、纯色秧鸡等92种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 圭亚那
nodes.summary: 圭亚那是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联285种鸟类，包括灰䳍、灰腿穴䳍、红脚穴䳍等285种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: Blood-colored woodpecker
nodes.summary: Blood-colored woodpecker（Blood-colored woodpecker，学名 Veniliornis sanguineus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 蘇利南
nodes.summary: 蘇利南是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联229种鸟类，包括红脚穴䳍、角叫鴨、绿翅雁等229种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=August 2022 |access-date=9 January 2023 }} It is found in Guyana and Suriname.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=August 2022 |access-date=9 January 2023 }} It is found in Guyana and Suriname.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Though some sources say that the blood-coloured woodpecker is found in "the Guianas", the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society has records only in Guyana and Suriname, and not French Guiana. It is a bird of the coastal lowlands, where it inhabits mangroves, swamp forest, and sometimes coffee plantations

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Though some sources say that the blood-coloured woodpecker is found in "the Guianas", the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society has records only in Guyana and Suriname, and not French Guiana. It is a bird of the coastal lowlands, where it inhabits mangroves, swamp forest, and sometimes coffee plantations

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is "[f]airly widespread in its range, but restricted to lowlands of the Guianas." Habitat loss "could become a threat"
